headline: Fula Network Hits 30 Active Nodes as DePIN Storage Gains Momentum
subtitle: Functionland's growing node count arrives as the broader decentralized storage sector enters a phase of measurable, revenue-driven maturity.
briefing: The Functionland FULA Network reported 30 active nodes this week, with live metrics visible at cloud.fx.land/stats — a steady infrastructure signal as the project advances its ambition to become a prominent decentralized storage platform. The timing is notable. <cite index="1-7">By early 2026, DePIN's combined market capitalization sits in the $910 billion range, larger than the oracles sector, while generating tens of millions in monthly on-chain revenue from actual services.</cite> Within that sector, storage is a proven vertical: <cite index="10-16">decentralized storage protocols like Filecoin aggregate unused hard drive space across thousands of nodes, offering enterprise-grade data persistence at costs 60–70% below centralized cloud providers.</cite> Functionland targets that same cost gap. <cite index="12-22,12-23">FxBlox, the project's hardware node, costs under $1 a month to run and only needs air for cooling — up to 2x cheaper than centralized providers and up to 10x cheaper than decentralized alternatives.</cite> The network's Q2 2026 roadmap focuses on <cite index="11-14">migrating larger companies and organizations' data from traditional cloud to the Fula ecosystem.</cite> With the DePIN storage category increasingly attracting enterprise buyers seeking cost relief from hyperscaler pricing, the metric to watch next is whether Functionland's node growth translates into verifiable storage demand at cloud.fx.land.
